info.name: FirstClub Membership Service
info.description:
Complete API collection for the FirstClub Membership Program.

## Quick Start
1. Start PostgreSQL: `docker-compose up -d`
2. Start app: `mvn clean spring-boot:run`
3. Run '1. Get Plans' to populate plan IDs
4. Run '2. Get Tiers' to populate tier IDs
5. Follow the numbered sequence in each folder

## Variables
- `baseUrl`: http://localhost:8080
- `userId`: auto-generated UUID
- Plan/Tier IDs: auto-populated from GET responses

item.name: 0. Setup

exec: // Generate a random UUID for testing
// Fallback: manual UUID generation if uuid module unavailable
var r = Math.random() * 16 | 0, v = c == 'x' ? r : (r & 0x3 | 0x8);

request.description: Dummy request that generates a userId. The actual user ID is set in the pre-request script.

item.description: Run these first to populate collection variables.

item.name: 1. Plans & Tiers (Catalog)

request.description: List all active membership plans (Monthly, Quarterly, Yearly). Auto-populates plan ID variables.
Get details of a specific plan by ID.
List all tiers with benefits and eligibility criteria. Auto-populates tier ID variables.
Get details of a specific tier including benefits and eligibility criteria.

item.description: Browse available plans and tiers. Run these first to auto-populate IDs.

item.name: 2. Subscribe

request.description: Subscribe with plan only. Tier is auto-calculated (defaults to Silver for new users).

exec: // Generate a fresh userId for this test
var r = Math.random() * 16 | 0, v = c == 'x' ? r : (r & 0x3 | 0x8);

request.description: Subscribe with plan + user-selected Silver tier. Silver has no criteria, so always succeeds.
Try subscribing again with same userId. EXPECTED TO FAIL with 409 Conflict.

exec: var r = Math.random() * 16 | 0, v = c == 'x' ? r : (r & 0x3 | 0x8);

request.description: Attempt to subscribe with Gold tier. EXPECTED TO FAIL with 400 because the new user has no orders and doesn't meet Gold criteria (10 orders + ₹3000 spend).
Subscribe with plan only. Tier is auto-calculated (defaults to Silver for new users).

item.description: Subscribe a user to a plan. Two flows: with user-selected tier or auto-calculated.

item.name: 3. Tier Eligibility & Evaluation

request.description: Check if user qualifies for Gold. Should fail — no orders yet.
Simulate 12 orders worth ₹5000. This exceeds Gold criteria (10 orders + ₹3000).
Check Gold eligibility again — should now pass.
Re-evaluate tier. Should promote from Silver → Gold based on order activity.
Add 18 more orders + ₹10000. Totals: 30 orders, ₹15000 (exceeds Platinum: 25 orders + ₹10000).
Evaluate again. Should promote Gold → Platinum.
Fire-and-forget tier evaluation. Returns 202 immediately, processes in background thread pool.

item.description: Check eligibility, simulate orders, then re-evaluate tier.

request.description: Combined view: subscription + tier + benefits. This is the main 'membership card' endpoint.
Full subscription history (includes upgraded, downgraded, cancelled records).
Upgrade from Monthly → Yearly. Old subscription is marked UPGRADED, new one created.
Downgrade from Yearly → Quarterly. Old subscription marked DOWNGRADED.
Try changing to same plan. EXPECTED TO FAIL with 400.
View full history — should show ACTIVE, UPGRADED, DOWNGRADED records.
Cancel active subscription. Status set to CANCELLED, reason recorded.

exec: pm.test('Status 404 - no active subscription', function() { pm.response.to.have.status(404); });

request.description: Try cancelling again. EXPECTED TO FAIL — no active subscription.
Check status after cancellation. Subscription is null but tier history remains.

item.name: 7. Demo Utilities

request.description: Add mock orders for a user. Values accumulate across calls.
Add user to a named cohort for cohort-based tier eligibility.
View mock order data for a user (order count, total value, cohorts).
Clear all mock order data for a user.

item.description: Helper endpoints for simulating order data. These are demo-only.

item.name: 8. Validation & Edge Cases

request.description: Missing required userId field. Should return validation error.
Non-existent plan ID. Should return 404.
Get membership for a user who never subscribed. Returns isMember=false.
Check eligibility for a non-existent tier. Should return 404.

item.description: Error scenarios for testing error handling.

item.name: 9. Free Delivery Checks

request.description: Re-evaluate tier. Should promote from Silver → Gold based on order activity.
Order is ₹300 but Silver needs ₹500 minimum for free delivery. Should fail.
Order is ₹600, above the ₹500 minimum. Should pass with 3 remaining uses.

exec: var r = Math.random() * 16 | 0, v = c == 'x' ? r : (r & 0x3 | 0x8);

request.description: Record 1st free delivery usage. Remaining: 2/3.
After 1 usage, should show 2 remaining.

exec: var r = Math.random() * 16 | 0, v = c == 'x' ? r : (r & 0x3 | 0x8);

request.description: Record 2nd free delivery usage. Remaining: 1/3.

exec: var r = Math.random() * 16 | 0, v = c == 'x' ? r : (r & 0x3 | 0x8);

request.description: Record 3rd free delivery usage. This exhausts the monthly limit.
All 3 free deliveries exhausted. Should show not eligible with limit reached.
Try to record a 4th free delivery. EXPECTED TO FAIL with 400.

item.description: Silver tier: free delivery on orders above ₹500, max 3/month.

item.name: 10. Discount Checks

request.description: ₹100 order, Silver needs ₹200 minimum for discount.

exec: // 5% of 5000 = 250, but capped at 200. Checkout calculates this.

request.description: ₹5000 order. 5% = ₹250 but capped at ₹200. Response shows value=5 and maxDiscountCap=200 for checkout to calculate.

item.name: 2.3 Check Discount - Unlimited usage (no limit on discounts)

request.description: Discount has no maxUsageLimit — unlimited uses per month.

item.description: Silver tier: 5% discount on orders above ₹200, max ₹200 off.

item.name: 11. Benefit Not in Tier
3.1 Check Priority Support (Silver doesn't have it)

request.description: Silver tier doesn't include PRIORITY_SUPPORT (only Platinum does).
Silver tier doesn't include CASHBACK_PERCENTAGE (only Platinum does).

item.description: Silver tier doesn't have PRIORITY_SUPPORT or CASHBACK.

request.description: Unknown user has no tier. Should return 404.
Non-existent benefit type. Should return 400.
Negative order value should fail the minimum order check.
